plan_section: Complete Forge feature: Steps 1-4 sound density engine, regression coverage, mobile/logo polish, and final Docker integration verification

plan_alignment.notes: The implemented feature matches the master plan: density-first metadata-only sound routing, AudioWorklet/fallback bed, public diagnostics, regression coverage, mobile bottom-sheet sound controls, same-origin Colorado Mesh logo assets, and Docker smoke coverage for the new worklet and static assets.

correctness_safety.notes: Sound remains opt-in and locked behind user gesture, Off mode avoids rendering, traffic ingestion occurs before accent dedupe/throttling, cleanup counters are bounded, upstream CoreScope audio is suppressed, and sound normalization excludes raw payload/message-body content. No secret-dependent behavior or runtime remote logo URLs were found.

code_quality.notes: Changes are localized to the overlay, tests, static assets, site metadata/constants, and Docker smoke script. The sound engine lifecycle, shell sheet behavior, CSS breakpoints, and smoke helpers are readable and consistent with existing project style.

completeness.notes: Automated verification is comprehensive: lint, typecheck, unit tests, full Chromium smoke, diff check, Docker build, and Docker smoke passed. Synthetic/browser tests cover burst density, privacy, modes, cleanup, mobile sheet geometry, analyzer/focus behavior, safe-area padding, touch targets, and icon assets. The only remaining gap is that the planned 5-10 minute audible listening pass was not performed; this should be recorded as a release caveat rather than a code-blocking issue.

patterns.notes: The implementation stays in the overlay/public/site layers, preserves existing __coloradoMeshSound API and localStorage keys, avoids vendor/CoreScope edits, uses same-origin static assets, and keeps user-facing branding aligned to Colorado Mesh.

integration.notes: The Docker smoke script now verifies /sound/denvermc-density-worklet.js and logo/favicon routes in addition to map overlay injection, orchestral assets, CoreScope APIs, WebSocket, and tile placeholder behavior. Site metadata, manifest, JSON-LD, BrandMark, overlay logo path, and Playwright expectations are aligned with the vendored assets.

flags.note: The final 5-10 minute audible listening pass was not performed. The synthetic/browser public-API burst coverage is acceptable for this code review gate, but release notes or final Forge summary should keep this as a validation caveat until an actual listening pass confirms subjective sound quality.
